posts.title: Welcome Lodg
posts.excerpt: AI workforce automates property management tasks, boosting efficiency across mid‑market.
posts.author: shuckerVC

body.text: AI Workforce for Property Management

Property management at the mid-market and long tail — some 60,000 U.S. property managers running 100–5,000 units — is structurally labor-bound: 40–200 units per full-time employee, at roughly 10% EBITDA margins. The labor model is breaking under wage inflation at the precise moment LLMs crossed the reliability threshold to execute multi-step workflows across the legacy stack — without a rip-and-replace.

The incumbents, meanwhile, just peaked. On May 28, 2026, Entrata filed for its IPO with the strongest numbers property management software has ever produced: 2.5 million units on platform — roughly 10% of U.S. multifamily — and $143.5M in revenue in the first quarter of 2026 alone. Entrata and AppFolio both ship AI offerings today, but their AI answers questions about the work; the work itself still routes to a human. Lodg's customers operate at 500+ units per FTE — with core workflows at 95%+ automation — on a book of business assembled in three months, entirely inbound.
The incumbents' AI talks. Lodg's works.
The system-of-record era just peaked with an IPO filing. The next $100B in property management doesn't go to whoever stores the data — it goes to whoever executes.
The Problem No Property Management Platform Ever Solved

The software era's answer to the labor problem was the system of record. AppFolio, Yardi, and Entrata built billion-dollar platforms that store the data — the leases, the ledgers, the work orders — and the industry pays $24B a year for them. But every new door under management still means more leasing calls, more application screens, more maintenance dispatches, more delinquency follow-ups. Labor scales linearly with the portfolio, and there is no operating leverage anywhere in the model.
The systems of record made the work more visible. They never made it smaller.
What Lodg Actually Does

Lodg is an AI workforce for property managers — agents that execute end-to-end workflows across the systems managers already run on. No rip-and-replace, no migration project, no new system to learn. The agents plug into the existing stack and start taking work off the team's plate.

Leasing and applications. From the first inquiry to a screened, decision-ready application — inbound calls and texts answered, showings coordinated, applicants screened and packaged. The workflows that consume entire teams run at 95%+ automation in production.

Maintenance. Requests come in at all hours; the agents answer, triage, resolve what can be resolved remotely, and dispatch what can't. One customer's first night live: a 2 a.m. maintenance call answered and resolved before anyone woke up — no work order ever created.

Renewals, payments, and delinquency. The most complex workflows in the business — multi-party negotiations between owners and tenants, comps, payment plans, collections conversations — handled end-to-end with a human stepping in only where judgment is required.
This isn't a copilot suggesting replies inside a system of record. The workforce is the product.
Why Now
Three forces are converging to make this company fundable right now.
The Numbers That Earned Our Conviction
We don't invest on narrative alone. Here's what the data showed:

The book of business compounded — without a dollar of outbound. In three months, Lodg went from first deployments to an exponentially growing customer base: every operator arrived inbound or by referral, and unsolicited interest is coming from category-adjacent CEOs. More telling is where the growth comes from — operators who already run on the incumbents' platforms. Lodg is winning the work layer on top of the systems of record, before the incumbents' own AI gets there.

The labor math is already validated — in production. The 500+ units per FTE isn't a projection; it's how Lodg's paying customers operate today, across their real portfolios. And the pricing matches the promise: Lodg charges for work completed — not for seats, not for licenses. The operator's cost scales with outcomes, and an agent that doesn't do the work doesn't get paid. For the operator, that's not just an efficiency gain — it's a different business model on both sides of the transaction.

The founding team matches the moment. CEO Jake Drutchas combines MIT engineering and business training with operating scar tissue: on the founding team at Wurthy, he scaled SMB finance-agent sales from zero to $1M+ ARR while running ops, finance, and customer success. CTO Amitav Chakravartty is a YC alum, former Director of Engineering at Cohesity, and co-founder/CTO of VaycayHero — he has built both venture-scale infrastructure and proptech specifically.
Our Thesis

At shuckerVC, we invest in AI-native B2B software that transforms traditional industries through outcome-based models and genuine technical moats. Lodg hits all three pillars: the product is agentic from the ground up rather than bolted onto a legacy system, it's priced on work completed rather than seats, and the moat compounds — every workflow the agents execute feeds back into how the next one runs, across every property and every market Lodg operates in. A competitor can copy the feature list in a quarter. They can't copy years of accumulated execution.
Lodg sells against the $100B property management labor market, not the $24B software market — and reaches the long tail of operators that enterprise platforms will never serve.
We're glad to be backing Jake, Amitav, and the Lodg team as they put property management on autopilot. We're hiring Lodg's Support Partner now — the role is open here.

posts.title: Welcome Cascade
posts.excerpt: Cascade builds AI construction graph, unlocking capital flow for builders.
posts.author: shuckerVC

body.text: Why We Invested in Cascade: Creating Value Through the First Construction Graph

In February 2026, the U.S. government allocated $2.1 billion to the Intel Ohio fab. Months later, $180 million of that landed with the Georgia DOT. Then Fulton County cut RFP 24-B-3891. Then a single subcontractor — one of thousands who could have qualified — won the work.
That chain happens thousands of times a year across the $2 trillion American construction economy. Until Cascade, no one had a structured map of it.

shuckerVC invested $400K at a $25M post-money cap. Cascade isn't replacing an incumbent — it's building the first construction graph. A structured, AI-traversable model of how American capital becomes American construction, with intelligent agents that traverse it on behalf of the firms that build.
The Problem No Database Ever Solved
Here's a number that should bother every AEC operator: 30 to 50 percent of capability-matched bid opportunities never reach the firms that should win them.

The American construction market is roughly $2 trillion in annual spend across 1.45 million firms, fragmented across hundreds of state and local jurisdictions with no federal equivalent of Deltek GovWin. The incumbents that own this layer — Dodge, ConstructConnect, BidNet, IMS — sell what they sold in 2019: a list of RFPs and a keyword search box. They digitized the database. They never digitized the intelligence.
The pain is existential, not operational. Cascade CEO Hannia Zia grew up watching her father's construction business fail because he couldn't find enough projects to bid on.
What Cascade Actually Does
Cascade is built on a single architectural insight: before you ship intelligent agents into a vertical, you build the structured semantic model of the vertical itself.

The construction graph. A machine-readable model of how American capital becomes American construction — federal programs, agencies, counties, owners, permits, jurisdictions, and the firms that build. The incumbents have spent two decades selling lists. Cascade is selling intelligence.

Frictionless onboarding. Cascade stands up a new AEC firm in minutes, not months. The platform learns what the firm does, where it works, what it's qualified for, and what it has won before — then matches the firm to opportunities it should be bidding on.

An agent fleet built for the work. Intelligent agents run on top of the graph — surfacing opportunities, drafting proposals, scoring win probability, structuring teaming arrangements, navigating compliance. The agents don't just observe the work. They do it.
This isn't AI bolted onto an RFP database. The structured model of the industry is the product.
Why Now

AEC AI adoption doubled year-over-year. BuiltWorlds tracks forty AI-native AEC solutions in 2026, up from a handful the year before. Cascade's customer roster already spans firms across architecture, data center development, and energy manufacturing.

Federal capital is reshaping where construction happens. CHIPS and IIJA have committed over a trillion dollars through 2028. AEC firms that can't trace capital flow from program to RFP at speed will lose work to firms that can.

The category has no entrenched competitor — yet. Dodge and ConstructConnect have not shipped production AI as of February 2026. The April 2026 Autodesk Construction Cloud roadmap shows no public AI-bid-matching signal. The window for the first construction graph is open today.
The Signals That Earned Our Conviction

Inbound pull, no outbound. Cascade has reached its first cohort of customers without paid marketing and without an SDR-led sales motion. Demand is finding the product, not the other way around. That is what category creation looks like before the category has a name.

Outcome-aligned economics. Cascade's revenue model grows with customer wins — when their customers win contracts through the platform, Cascade benefits. That replaces the per-seat SaaS pricing the AI era is rendering obsolete and matches our outcome-based-pricing thesis.

Two founders, working platform. Cascade is two co-founders plus a small team of interns and contractors. They have shipped a working agent platform on top of a structured industry graph in three months. That kind of velocity is what vertical AI demands and what traditional engineering org charts cannot produce.

The founding team matches the moment. CEO Hannia Zia scaled Google Pay from zero to 100 million users. CTO Joana Ferreira is ex-Google ML and was a founding engineer at trading apps — the most articulate technical founder we encountered at a16z Speedrun SR006.
Our Thesis
At shuckerVC, we invest in AI-native B2B software that transforms traditional industries through outcome-based pricing and genuine technical moats. Cascade hits every dimension.

The moats are stacked and self-reinforcing. The construction graph is a methodology moat — building it requires industry depth, not just prompt engineering. Every customer interaction labels the dataset, so the moat deepens with each cohort. Like Palantir's Foundry, the value compounds with use.
We're glad to be backing Hannia, Joana, and the Cascade team as they build the structured map of how America gets built. We're hiring Cascade's Support Partner now — the role is open here.

posts.title: AI is Eating Software
posts.excerpt: Just as cloud and mobile once disrupted enterprise software, AI is now poised to be the next transformative force in the B2B SaaS landscape. With 60% of Y-Combinator's recent startups focused on AI, the potential for disruption is evident.
posts.author: JP Persico

body.text: The introduction of the Macintosh computer was a watershed moment in personal computing, setting new standards and reshaping user experiences. Similarly, the enterprise B2B software landscape was profoundly disrupted by the cloud and mobile revolutions, giving birth to the B2B SaaS industry we recognize today. Now, as we stand on the cusp of an AI-driven transformation, it feels reminiscent of that Macintosh moment. AI is poised to be the next significant disruptor, promising to redefine the B2B SaaS space. This isn't just another technological wave; it's a foundational shift that redefines business models, value propositions, and the very essence of enterprise solutions.
The Data Speaks: Y-Combinator's AI Surge

A telling sign of this AI-driven transformation is evident in the composition of Y-Combinator's recent batch. An impressive 60% of the startups from YC's S23 batch are rooted in AI and machine learning. This isn't a fleeting trend; it's a testament to the industry's recognition of AI's potential and the entrepreneurial drive to harness it.
Disruption of the Disruptors

The B2B SaaS landscape, which itself emerged from the disruption of traditional enterprise software by cloud and mobile technologies, is now witnessing another wave of disruption from AI-first startups. These new entrants, backed by powerful algorithms and deep learning models, are challenging even the most established SaaS giants. The message is clear: adapt to the AI era or risk obsolescence.
Reimagining Enterprise Solutions

AI's potential isn't limited to enhancing existing solutions. It's about re-envisioning entire enterprise workflows. For instance, Postscript's AI-driven approach to SMS marketing is not just an enhancement; it's a paradigm shift in how businesses engage with customers. Another compelling example is Ironclad, a company that's revolutionizing contract life cycle management through AI. Ironclad's AI capabilities automatically analyze contracts, flag areas that require a thorough review, and provide suggestions on how to negotiate based on legal-approved guidelines. What once involved countless meetings and document revisions is now streamlined by AI.
AI Co-Pilots: The New Norm

In sectors like finance, insurance, and brokerage, AI is transitioning from being a backend tool to a frontline co-pilot. It's not just about automation; it's about enhancing human capabilities, offering insights, and driving decision-making.
A Strategic Imperative

For established B2B SaaS players, the rise of AI is both a challenge and an opportunity. It's not just about integrating AI modules into existing platforms; it's about a strategic realignment. Companies need to rethink their core value propositions, workflows, and customer engagement strategies in the context of AI.
Beyond the Hype: AI's Tangible Value

While AI often finds itself surrounded by hype, its tangible impact in the B2B SaaS space is undeniable. Whether it's predictive analytics, intelligent automation, or deep personalization, AI is not just a feature; it's becoming the core of value creation.
Harness the moment with shuckerVC

In this transformative era, where AI is reshaping the B2B SaaS landscape, there lies a pivotal moment for visionary startups. At shuckerVC, we're not just observers of this shift; we're active participants. As a Bay Area seed fund, our focus is laser-sharp: backing AI-powered B2B software companies in the US. We believe in the power of AI and are deeply committed to supporting the founders leading this revolution.

The journey of innovation is demanding. Founders, in their drive to innovate and solve hard problems with scarce resources, often find themselves wearing multiple hats and juggling myriad roles. This multitasking, while commendable, can slow their momentum, diverting attention from core objectives and diluting their focus. shuckerVC understands this challenge and is here to help. Our Ops Team allows founders to delegate whole back-office tasks to our team, offering these services at cost, ensuring they can maintain their focus and accelerate their vision.

With our specialized focus and a vast network that includes relationships with over 400 VC firms and top accelerators, we're continuously seeking the technical founders who will define the next chapter of B2B SaaS, powered by AI.

posts.title: Our Support Partner Model
posts.excerpt: The bottleneck for early founders isn't capital — it's operational drag. How we embed a full-time operator in every portfolio company, at cost and with zero dilution.
posts.author: shuckerVC

body.text: At shuckerVC, we believe the biggest bottleneck for technical founders isn't capital — it's operational drag. That's why we built the Support Partner Model: a dedicated, full-time operational partner embedded in each portfolio company to handle everything outside of product and engineering.
This is our core differentiator as a fund, and it's the reason founders choose us — even when they have competing term sheets on the table.
How It Works

When a company joins the shuckerVC portfolio, we deploy a Support Partner — a full-time W2 employee hired through shuckerVC's separate LLC. The portfolio company pays their salary at cost, with zero markup from shuckerVC and zero equity dilution for the founder.

Support Partners are embedded within each portfolio company, working directly with the leadership team through its highest-growth phase. From day one, they implement systems and processes that prevent organizational terminal velocity, ensuring no single person becomes the constraint to growth. As the company scales, these systems enable a smooth transition to functional leads who sustain and extend that velocity.
We help companies break through terminal velocity by building the system that prevents it.
What Support Partners Do

Support Partners handle everything that isn't product or engineering. People Operations: recruiting, hiring, HR, payroll, benefits administration, onboarding, and performance reviews — at Algorized, a single Support Partner hired 12 people in 9 months. Financial Operations: monthly book closing, expense management, financial forecasting, accounting coordination, vendor payments, and revenue collection, serving as the interface between the company and its bookkeeping vendor. Growth & Marketing: LinkedIn management, website management, event coordination, PR support, and investor update preparation. Administrative & Compliance: office operations, vendor management, SOC2 compliance coordination, legal document collection, and Series A prep support.
Compensation & Alignment

Rather than taking equity from portfolio companies, Support Partners receive an allocation from shuckerVC's GP carry pool. This structure is deliberate. For founders: no equity dilution — you get experienced operational talent at cost, not at the $240K+ market rate for a chief of staff. For Support Partners: alignment with overall fund performance rather than the fate of a single company — they succeed when the entire portfolio succeeds. For LPs: direct value creation in portfolio companies that accelerates returns across the fund.
Talent Pipeline

We recruit Support Partners who are natural problem solvers — specifically those transitioning out of consulting who want hands-on startup experience but lack access to early-stage companies. We source candidates through non-public job platforms and present a bench of 4–5 vetted candidates to each founder for interviews and selection. What we look for: 2 to 3 years of consulting experience; an AI-native mindset (we can teach enterprise skills, but it's harder to teach AI orchestration); career pivoters with strong operational instincts; and people who thrive as problem solvers and orchestrators, not just executors.
Two Career Paths for Support Partners

Company Hire: the portfolio company hires them directly as a permanent W2 employee — we do not block or restrict this natural transition. Redeploy: they stay with shuckerVC and move to the next portfolio company, building deep cross-company operational expertise. This creates a talent recycling system across our target portfolio of 20 companies, where operational knowledge compounds across the fund.
Proven Results: Algorized

Before: 4 people, early-stage revenue. After: 15 people, substantially higher revenue, raising a strong up-round from its seed valuation. Impact: the Support Partner hired 12 people in 9 months and managed all HR, office operations, and marketing — freeing the CEO to focus exclusively on technology and fundraising. Outcome: Algorized chose shuckerVC over 3 other term sheets specifically because of the Support Partner offering.
Why Founders Choose Us
Founders choose shuckerVC over other term sheets specifically for the Support Partner offering.

Technical founders face a fundamental tension: every hour spent on HR, finance, compliance, and vendor management is an hour not spent on product or customers. Our model resolves that tension completely. For founders, the value proposition is simple: get an experienced operational partner at cost, with no equity dilution, who is fully aligned with your success through fund carry.

posts.title: Why we backed Atlas: monetizing impact, not tokens
posts.excerpt: As compute costs collapse, durable SaaS won't meter tokens — it'll price the outcomes it delivers. The thesis behind our investment in Atlas.
posts.author: Graham Siegel

body.text: The end of token metering
As compute costs collapse, pricing software by tokens consumed becomes a race to the bottom. Durable companies won't meter the cost of the work — they'll price the value of the outcome it produces.
Atlas builds the layer that makes that possible: dynamic, outcome-based pricing and monetization for AI-native SaaS.
Why we invested

Michael is a three-time founder who has lived the pricing problem from the inside. The team is building exactly where we believe the market is heading, and they're doing it with the operating discipline we look for.
It's a classic shuckerVC investment: a technical founder, a market ripe for change, and an oversubscribed round alongside a top lead investor.

posts.title: Algorized raises its Series A
posts.excerpt: Our edge-AI perception company closes its Series A alongside Amazon — bringing robots that sense and anticipate people to the factory floor.
posts.author: shuckerVC

body.text: Algorized, our edge-AI perception company, has closed its Series A alongside Amazon.
The company builds perception that lets robots sense and anticipate people on the factory floor — making industrial automation safer and more adaptive.

We backed Algorized at seed and embedded a Support Partner to handle finance ops and Series A prep, so founder Natalya Lopareva could stay focused on the technology. We could not be happier to see the milestone.
